texto: The dirty secret she’d learned about grief was that nobody wanted to hear about your loss a week after the funeral. People you’d once considered friends would turn their heads in church or cross to another side of a shopping mall to avoid the contamination of your suffering. “You might imagine I’m coping day by day,” she murmured. “But it’s more a case of hour by hour, and during my worst times, minute by minute.
explicacao: Citação de Susan Dormady Eisenberg sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The dirty secret she’d learned about grief was that nobody wanted to hear about your loss a week …” articula amizade, coping, grief em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como luto e termino.
